title: Genghis Khan dies
subtitle: Founder and first khan of the Mongol Empire
fact: Genghis Khan, also known as Chinggis Khan, was the founder and first khan of the Mongol Empire.

title: Alī ibn Abī Ṭālib dies
fact: Ali ibn Abi Talib was the fourth Rashidun caliph who ruled from 656 CE until his assassination in 661, as well as the first Shia Imam.

title: Joan of Arc dies
fact: Joan of Arc is a patron saint of France, honored as a defender of the French nation for her role in the siege of Orléans and her insistence on the coronation of Charles VII of France during the Hundred Years' War.

title: Richard III of England dies
fact: Richard III was King of England from 26 June 1483 until his death in 1485.

title: Charlemagne dies
fact: Charlemagne was King of the Franks from 768, King of the Lombards from 774, and Emperor of what is now known as the Carolingian Empire from 800.

title: William the Conqueror dies
fact: William the Conqueror, sometimes called William the Bastard, was the first Norman king of England, reigning from 1066 until his death.

title: Henry VI of England dies
fact: Henry VI was King of England from 1422 to 1461 and again from 1470 to 1471, and claimant to the French throne from 1422 to 1453 under the terms of the Treaty of Troyes.

title: Richard I of England dies
fact: Richard I, known as Richard the Lionheart or Richard Cœur de Lion because of his reputation as a great military leader and warrior, was King of England from 1189 until his death in 1199.

title: Alfred the Great dies
subtitle: King of Wessex
fact: Alfred the Great was King of the West Saxons from 871 to 886, and King of the Anglo-Saxons from 886 until his death in 899.

title: John, King of England dies
fact: John was King of England from 1199 until his death in 1216.

title: Henry II of England dies
fact: Henry II was King of England from 1154 until his death in 1189.

title: Edward IV of England dies
fact: Edward IV was King of England from 4 March 1461 to 3 October 1470, then again from 11 April 1471 until his death in 1483.

title: Umar ibn Al-Khattāb dies
fact: Umar ibn al-Khattab, also spelled Omar, was the second Rashidun caliph, ruling from August 634 until his assassination in 644.

title: Thomas Aquinas dies
subtitle: Italian Dominican friar, philosopher, Catholic priest, and Doctor of the Church
fact: Thomas Aquinas was an Italian Dominican friar and priest, theologian, and philosopher.

title: Edward III of England dies
fact: Edward III, also known as Edward of Windsor before accession, was King of England from January 1327 until his death in 1377.

title: Edward II of England dies
fact: Edward II, also known as Edward of Caernarfon or Caernarvon, was King of England from 1307 until he was deposed in January 1327.

title: Rumi dies
subtitle: Iranian poet
fact: Jalāl al-Dīn Muḥammad Rūmī, commonly known as Rumi, was a Sufi mystic, poet, and founder of the Islamic brotherhood known as the Mevlevi Order.

title: Edward I of England dies
fact: Edward I, also known as Edward Longshanks and the Hammer of the Scots, was King of England from 1272 to 1307.

title: Mehmed II dies
subtitle: Seventh Sultan of the Ottoman Empire
fact: Mehmed II, commonly known as Mehmed the Conqueror, was the sultan of the Ottoman Empire twice, from August 1444 to September 1446 and then later from February 1451 to May 1481.

title: Dante Alighieri dies
subtitle: Florentine poet, writer, and philosopher
fact: Dante Alighieri, widely known mononymously as Dante, was an Italian poet, writer, and philosopher.

title: Hasan ibn Ali dies
fact: Hasan ibn Ali was an Alid political and religious leader.

title: Marco Polo dies
subtitle: Venetian explorer and merchant who travelled through Asia
fact: Marco Polo was a Venetian merchant, explorer, and writer who travelled through Asia along the Silk Road between 1271 and 1295.

title: Henry III of England dies
fact: Henry III, also known as Henry of Winchester, was King of England, Lord of Ireland and Duke of Aquitaine from 1216 until his death in 1272.

title: Cnut the Great dies
subtitle: King of Denmark, Norway, and England
fact: Cnut, also known as Canute and with the epithet the Great, was King of England from 1016, King of Denmark from 1018, and King of Norway from 1028 until his death in 1035.

title: Hildegard of Bingen dies
subtitle: German Benedictine abbess, polymath, mystic and Doctor of Church
fact: Hildegard of Bingen OSB, also known as the Sibyl of the Rhine, was a German Benedictine abbess and polymath active as a writer, composer, philosopher, mystic, visionary, and as a medical writer and practitioner of the Catholic Church during the High Middle Ages.

title: Leif Erikson dies
subtitle: Norse explorer
fact: Leif Erikson, also known as Leif the Lucky, was a Norse explorer who is thought to have been the first European to set foot on continental America, approximately half a millennium before Christopher Columbus.

title: Avicenna dies
subtitle: Persian polymath, physician and philosopher
fact: Ibn Sina, commonly known in the West as Avicenna, was a preeminent philosopher and physician of the Muslim world.

title: Geoffrey Chaucer dies
subtitle: 14Th century English poet and author
fact: Geoffrey Chaucer was an English poet, writer and civil servant best known for The Canterbury Tales.

title: Muḥammad ibn Musa al-Khwarizmi dies
subtitle: Persian mathematician and astronomer
fact: Muhammad ibn Musa al-Khwarizmi, or simply al-Khwarizmi was a mathematician active during the Islamic Golden Age, who produced Arabic-language works in mathematics, astronomy, and geography.

title: Harald III of Norway dies
fact: Harald Sigurdsson, also known as Harald III and given the epithet Hardrada in the sagas, was King of Norway from 1046 to 1066.

title: Johannes Gutenberg dies
subtitle: German inventor and craftsman
fact: Johannes Gensfleisch zur Laden zum Gutenberg was a German inventor and craftsman who invented the movable-type printing press.

title: Ibn Battuta dies
subtitle: 14Th century Muslim Maghrebi scholar and explorer
fact: Ibn Battuta was a Maghrebi Muslim traveller, explorer and scholar.

title: Charles VI of France dies
fact: Charles VI, nicknamed the Beloved and in the 19th century, the Mad, was King of France from 1380 until his death in 1422.

title: Frederick II, Holy Roman Emperor dies
subtitle: King of Sicily, Holy Roman Emperor
fact: Frederick II was King of Sicily from 1198, King of Germany from 1212, King of Italy and Holy Roman Emperor from 1220, and King of Jerusalem from 1225 to 1228.

title: Louis IX of France dies
fact: Louis IX, also known as Saint Louis, was King of France from 1226 until his death in 1270.

title: Belisarius dies
fact: Flavius Belisarius was a military commander of the Byzantine Empire under Emperor Justinian I.

title: Charles VII of France dies
subtitle: Monarch of the House of Valois who ruled as King of France
fact: Charles VII, called the Victorious or the Well-Served, was King of France from 1422 to his death in 1461.

title: Charles Martel dies
subtitle: Frankish military and political leader
fact: Charles Martel, Martel being an Old French sobriquet meaning "The Hammer", was a Frankish political and military leader who, as Duke and Prince of the Franks and Mayor of the Palace, was the de facto ruler of the Franks from 718 until his death.

title: Clovis I dies
subtitle: First king of the Franks
fact: Clovis I was the first Frankish king to unite the Franks, changing the form of leadership from a group of petty kings to rule by a single king, and ensuring that the kingship was passed down to his heirs.

title: Zheng He dies
subtitle: Chinese mariner, explorer, and diplomat
fact: Zheng He was a Chinese explorer, admiral, diplomat, and eunuch from the early Ming dynasty, who is often regarded as the greatest admiral in Chinese history.

title: Omar Khayyám dies
subtitle: Persian mathematician and poet
fact: Omar Khayyam (1048–1131) was a Persian poet and polymath, known for his contributions to mathematics, astronomy, philosophy, and Persian literature.

title: Skanderbeg dies
subtitle: Albanian noble and military commander
fact: Gjergj Kastrioti was an Albanian nobleman and military leader who led the League of Lezhë in the Ottoman-Albanian Wars until his death.

title: Charles IV dies
subtitle: Holy Roman Emperor
fact: Charles IV was Holy Roman Emperor from 1355 until his death in 1378.

title: Ibn Khaldun dies
subtitle: Arab historiographer and historian
fact: Ibn Khaldun was an Arab scholar, historian, philosopher, and sociologist.

title: Pepin the Short dies
fact: Pepin the Short was King of the Franks from 751 until his death in 768.

title: Ertuğrul dies
subtitle: Ottoman ruler, father of Osman I
fact: Ertuğrul or Ertuğrul Ghazi was a 13th-century uch bey (marcher-lord), who was the father of Osman I.

title: Murad II dies
subtitle: Sixth Sultan of the Ottoman Empire
fact: Murad II was twice the sultan of the Ottoman Empire, from 1421 to 1444 and from 1446 to 1451.

title: Murad I dies
subtitle: Third Ottoman sultan
fact: Murad I, nicknamed Hüdavendigâr, was the sultan of the Ottoman Empire from 1362 to 1389.

title: Petrarch dies
subtitle: Italian scholar and poet
fact: Francis Petrarch was an Italian scholar and poet of the early Italian Renaissance, as well as one of the earliest humanists.

title: Erik the Red dies
subtitle: Explorer and founder of the first viking settlement in Greenland
fact: Erik Thorvaldsson, known as Erik the Red, was a Norse explorer, described in medieval and Icelandic saga sources as having founded the first European settlement in Greenland.

title: Louis the Pious dies
subtitle: King of the Franks
fact: Louis the Pious, also called the Fair and the Debonaire, was King of the Franks and co-emperor with his father, Charlemagne, from 813.

title: Charles the Bald dies
fact: Charles the Bald was king of West Francia (843–77), king of Italy (875–77) and emperor of the Carolingian Empire (875–77).

title: Theodoric the Great dies
subtitle: King of the Germanic Ostrogoths and ruler of Italy
fact: Theodoric the Great, also called the Amal, was king of the Ostrogoths (475–526), and ruler of the Ostrogothic Kingdom of Italy between 493 and 526, regent of the Visigoths (511–526), and a patrician of the Eastern Roman Empire.

title: Hasan-i Sabbah dies
subtitle: The founder of the Ismaili state of Alamut and the Imam of the Nizari Ismailis
fact: Hasan-i Sabbah also known as Hasan I of Alamut, was a religious and military leader, founder of the Nizari Ismai'li sect, Order of Assassins, as well as the Nizari Ismaili state, ruling from 1090 to 1124 AD.

title: Averroes dies
subtitle: Arab Andalusian Muslim writer and philosopher
fact: Ibn Rushd, Latinized as Averroes, was an Andalusian polymath and jurist who was proficient in a variety of intellectual fields, including philosophy, theology, medicine, astronomy, physics, psychology, mathematics, neurology, Islamic jurisprudence and law, and linguistics.

title: Ibn al-Haytham dies
subtitle: Persian physicist, mathematician and astronomer
fact: Ibn al-Haytham, Latinized as Alhazen, was a mathematician, astronomer, and physicist of the Islamic Golden Age from present-day Iraq.

title: Vladimir the Great dies
subtitle: Grand Prince of Kiev
fact: Vladimir I Sviatoslavich or Volodymyr I Sviatoslavych, given the epithet "the Great", was Prince of Novgorod from 970 and Grand Prince of Kiev from 978 until his death in 1015.

title: Xuanzang dies
subtitle: Chinese Buddhist monk and scholar
fact: Xuanzang, born Chen Hui or Chen Yi, also known by his Sanskrit Dharma name Mokṣadeva, was a 7th-century Chinese Buddhist monk, scholar, traveller, and translator.

title: Jan van Eyck dies
subtitle: Flemish painter
fact: Jan van Eyck was a Flemish painter active in Bruges who was one of the early innovators of what became known as Early Netherlandish painting, and one of the supreme figures of the Early Northern Renaissance.

title: Donatello dies
subtitle: Early Renaissance Italian sculptor
fact: Donato di Niccolò di Betto Bardi, known mononymously as Donatello, was an Italian sculptor of the Renaissance period.

title: Amir Khusrau dies
subtitle: Indian poet, writer, singer and scholar
fact: Abu'l Hasan Yamīn ud-Dīn Khusrau, better known as Amīr Khusrau, sometimes spelled as, Amir Khusrow or Amir Khusro, was an Indo-Persian Sufi singer, musician, court poet and scholar, who lived during the period of the Delhi Sultanate.

title: Louis VII of France dies
fact: Louis VII, was King of France from 1137 to 1180.

title: Giotto dies
subtitle: Italian painter and architect
fact: Giotto di Bondone, known mononymously as Giotto, was an Italian painter and architect from Florence during the Late Middle Ages.

title: Roger Bacon dies
subtitle: English polymath, philosopher and friar
fact: Roger Bacon, also known by the scholastic accolade Doctor Mirabilis, was a medieval English polymath, philosopher, scientist, theologian and Franciscan friar who placed considerable emphasis on the study of nature through empiricism.

title: Basava dies
subtitle: Hindu philosopher, statesman, Kannada Bhakti poet of Lingayatism
fact: Basava (1131–1196), also called Basavēśvara and Basavaṇṇa, was an Indian philosopher, poet, Lingayat social reformer in the Shiva-focused bhakti movement, and a Hindu Shaivite social reformer during the reign of the Kalyani Chalukya and the Kalachuri Dynasty.

title: Li Bai dies
subtitle: Classical Chinese poet of the Tang dynasty
fact: Li Bai also known by his courtesy name of Taibai (太白) was a Chinese poet acclaimed as one of the best and most important poets of the Tang dynasty, and even in the whole of Chinese poetry.

title: Peter Abelard dies
subtitle: French scholastic philosopher, theologian and preeminent logician
fact: Peter Abelard was a medieval French scholastic, philosopher, leading logician, theologian, teacher, musician, composer, and poet.

title: Wat Tyler dies
subtitle: English rebel
fact: Wat Tyler was a leader of the 1381 Peasants' Revolt in England.

title: Brendan the Navigator dies
subtitle: Irish monastic saint
fact: Brendan of Clonfert is one of the early Irish monastic saints and one of the Twelve Apostles of Ireland.

title: Murasaki Shikibu dies
subtitle: Japanese novelist and poet
fact: Murasaki Shikibu, or Shijo, was a Japanese novelist, poet and lady-in-waiting at the Imperial court in the Heian period.

title: Al-Mukhtar dies
subtitle: Pro-Alid Arab revolutionary
fact: Al-Mukhtar ibn Abi Ubayd al-Thaqafi was an Arab Pro-Alid revolutionary based in Kufa, who led a rebellion against the Umayyad Caliphate in 685 and ruled over most of Iraq for eighteen months during the Second Fitna.

title: Afonso I of Portugal dies
fact: Dom Afonso I nicknamed "the Conqueror", "the Founder" and "the Great" by the Portuguese, was the first king of Portugal, from 26 July 1139 until his death on 6 December 1185.

title: Abu Bakr al-Razi dies
subtitle: Persian polymath, physician, chemist and philosopher
fact: Abū Bakr al-Rāzī, also known as Rhazes, 864 or 865 – 925 or 935 CE, was a Persian physician, philosopher and alchemist who lived during the Islamic Golden Age.

title: Muhammad al-Jawad dies
subtitle: Ninth of the Twelve Imams of Twelver Shi'ism
fact: Muhammad ibn Ali al-Jawad was a descendant of the Islamic prophet Muhammad and the ninth of the Twelve Imams, succeeding his father, Ali al-Rida.

title: Nasir al-Din al-Tusi dies
subtitle: Persian astronomer
fact: Muḥammad ibn Muḥammad ibn al-Ḥasan al-Ṭūsī, also known as Naṣīr al-Dīn al-Ṭūsī or simply as (al-)Tusi, was a Persian polymath, architect, philosopher, physician, scientist, and theologian.

title: Abbas ibn Abd al-Muttalib dies
subtitle: Paternal uncle of Muhammad
fact: Al-Abbas ibn Abd al-Muttalib was a paternal uncle and sahabi (companion) of the Islamic prophet Muhammad, just three years older than his nephew.

title: Leon Battista Alberti dies
subtitle: Italian architect and writer
fact: Leon Battista Alberti was an Italian Renaissance humanist author, artist, architect, poet, priest, linguist, philosopher, and cryptographer; he epitomised the nature of those identified now as polymaths.

title: Nizam al-Mulk dies
subtitle: Persian Seljuk scholar, politician, vizier and court official
fact: Abū ʿAlī al-Ḥasan ibn ʿAlī Ṭūsī, better known by his honorific title of Niẓām al-Mulk, was a Persian Sunni scholar, jurist, and vizier of the Seljuk Empire.

title: Babak Khorramdin dies
subtitle: Iranian revolutionary leader
fact: Bābak Khorramdin was one of the main Iranian revolutionary leaders of the Azerbaijani Khorram-Dinān, which was a local freedom movement fighting the Abbasid Caliphate.

title: Dmitry Donskoy dies
subtitle: Prince of Moscow
fact: Dmitry Ivanovich Donskoy was Prince of Moscow from 1359 and Grand Prince of Vladimir from 1363 until his death.

title: Zhu Xi dies
fact: Zhu Xi, formerly romanized Chu Hsi, was a Chinese philosopher, historian, politician, poet, and calligrapher of the Southern Song dynasty.

title: Rashid-al-Din Hamadani dies
subtitle: Persian physician and historian
fact: Rashīd al-Dīn Ṭabīb was a statesman, historian, and physician in Ilkhanate Iran.

title: Shinran dies
subtitle: Japanese Buddhist monk and founder of the Jōdo Shinshū sect
fact: Shinran was a key Japanese Buddhist figure of the Kamakura Period who is regarded as the founder of the Jōdo Shinshū school of Japanese Buddhism.

title: Ingólfr Arnarson dies
subtitle: Viking settler of Iceland
fact: Ingólfr Arnarson, in some sources named Björnólfsson, is commonly recognized as the first Icelander, when he became the first Norse settler of Iceland, together with his wife Hallveig Fróðadóttir and foster brother Hjǫrleifr Hróðmarsson.

title: Hunayn ibn Ishaq dies
subtitle: Arab Christian scholar, physician and scientist
fact: Hunayn ibn Ishaq al-Ibadi was an influential Iraqi Arab Nestorian Christian translator, scholar, physician, and scientist.

title: Giovanni da Pian del Carpine dies
subtitle: Roman Catholic archbishop and traveller
fact: Giovanni da Pian del Carpine OFM was a medieval Italian diplomat, Catholic archbishop, explorer and one of the first Europeans to enter the court of the Great Khan of the Mongol Empire.

title: Abul Hasan ash-Shadhili dies
subtitle: Founder of the Shadhili order
fact: Abu al-Hasan al-Shadhili also known as Sheikh al-Shadhili was an influential Moroccan Islamic scholar and Sufi, founder of the Shadhili Sufi order.

title: Qiu Chuji dies
subtitle: Taoist leader
fact: Qiu Chuji, courtesy name Tongmi (通密), also known by his Taoist name Master Changchun, was a renowned Taoist master from late Southern Song/Jin dynasty and a famous disciple of Wang Chongyang, the founder of Quanzhen School.

title: Romanos the Melodist dies
subtitle: Greek hymnographer
fact: Romanos the Melodist was a Byzantine hymnographer and composer, who is a central early figure in the history of Byzantine music.

title: Gerard de Ridefort dies
subtitle: Knight Templar
fact: Gérard de Ridefort, also called Gerard de Ridefort, was Grand Master of the Knights Templar from the end of 1184 and until his death in 1189.
